rulings.Case Title: AEGIS ASSET MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication; And Joinder In All Supporting Documents Filed Therewith, Including Separate Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts, Declarations, And Compendium Of Evidence; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Lisa Hiraide; Exhibits
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 21, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT CIM GROUP, L. P. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION; AND JOINDER IN ALL SUPPORTING DOCUMENTS FILED THEREWITH, INCLUDING SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS, DECLARATIONS, AND COMPENDIUM OF EVIDENCE; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES; DECLARATION OF LISA HIRAIDE; EXHIBITS is continued to March 14, 2025. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL MUSLEH VS. RICKEY JACKSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 21, 2025 line 5. DEFENDANT RICKEY JACKSON MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is GRANTED. Defendant to (1) post with the Court $4,734 (daily damages caused by the delay in responding to the complaint) no later than February 21, 2025; (2) upon posting Defendant to file an answer no later than 4:30 p.m. on February 21, 2025; (3) Defendant and/or his counsel to remit to Plaintiff's counsel $1,972.35 in fees and costs no later than March 28, 2025; declaration of compliance to be filed no later than April 4, 2025. Hearing Required. If the Court were to accept Mr. Bott's position (See Declaration of Bott, at paragraph 4) that the default was entered due to the fault of attorneys at EDC (apparently including attorneys who had no direct involvement with this case prior to the default, but are involved in some unspecified manner with the legal clinic) the Court will consider sanctioning each attorney employed at EDC per CCP 473(c)(1)(B). Mr. Bott to be present at the hearing with a list of all attorneys and their bar numbers. If Mr. Bott's position is accepted, the Court will be reporting each attorney to the Bar in conjunction with the error attributable to the attorneys at EDC per Mr. Bott's argument and declaration. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
